best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest contributor: 31 trades, $2419.4 PnL, $252.9/hour, no slow >=12h, worst MAE -403.2. It is productive but already carries medium tail risk, so avoid broad gates that cut its throughput.

charm_pin_pullback_long has 29 trades, $1077.4 PnL, $208.9/hour, median hold 3.0 minutes, and worst MAE only -335.75 in the latest run. Extended-VWAP winners are real, so blunt VWAP-extension pruning is dangerous.

charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 5 trades but $988 PnL, $234.9/hour, median TP 12, no slow trades, and worst MAE -357. It remains small-sample but repeatedly pays when wall-breakaway geometry is clean.

call_wall_acceptance_long produced $640.5 from 2 trades and $290.3/hour, but it owns the global -655.2 MAE and a 120-minute worst-tail hold. The edge exists, but risk-adjusted research should contain early-realization/tail exposure rather than expand sample first.
breakout_long_1dte produced 20 trades, $1241.8 PnL, no slow trades, and worst MAE -386.75. Prior direct tightening hurt objective, so keep baseline 1DTE breakout behavior stable.

squeeze_long delivered 10 trades, $774 PnL, $200.3/hour, no slow trades, and best profile worst MAE among active VALID_0DTE edges at -225. It is a clean edge to preserve while risk-reducing elsewhere.

broken_edges.evidence: 2 trades generated $640.5, but average MAE was -333.68 and worst MAE -655.2. The worst trade had good early favorable movement yet only reached TP after 120 minutes and deep heat.
broken_edges.problem: Tail-heavy convex profile; high PnL masks large hidden MAE and long capital tie-up.

broken_edges.action: do not direct-tighten existing breakout profile
broken_edges.evidence: Prior 1DTE quality-gate experiment cut score by -594 and net profit by -558.5 while worsening worst MAE. Latest 1DTE baseline is profitable and stable.
broken_edges.problem: Direct stricter quality gates on breakout_long_1dte have negative prior evidence.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE produced only 5 trades and $162.8 total. breakout_long_no_0dte stale trades had $40.5/hour and -250.25 worst MAE, while the single EXPIRED_0DTE trade was very efficient.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE fallback states are heterogeneous and under-sampled; stale fallback is weak, expired fallback is not proven bad.

broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long remain routed/configured but produced zero trades or are disabled. No current evidence supports optimizing them under a risk-reducer track.
broken_edges.problem: Dead or dormant active-router profiles with no latest-run contribution.

broken_edges.evidence: Top hidden MAE signatures retain only 70-89% PnL and often still leave worst MAE at -655.2. Some signatures would remove many good trades or the whole 1DTE edge.
broken_edges.problem: Categorical or broad threshold filters do not cleanly isolate tail MAE without excessive PnL loss.

market_regime_read: Short 12.3-day, high-win-rate, take-profit-only long scalper regime. Realized losses are absent and slow >=12h trades are zero, so the true objective risk is hidden MAE and delayed TP recovery, not realized loss rate. Current leader-family score comes from preserving fast VALID_0DTE pullback/charm/squeeze edges plus 1DTE fallback. The latest run improved raw PnL versus robust leaders but worsened drawdown/worst MAE: run_044/run_047 worst MAE -655.2 and drawdown 0.7% versus robust run_029 family worst MAE -513.4 and drawdown 0.4%. Risk-reducer research should target the call-wall/extended-VWAP near-miss tail while protecting pullback_long, charm_pin_pullback_long, squeeze_long, and breakout_long_1dte throughput.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Path-aware containment for the call-wall convex tail where static entry features are ambiguous.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Could overfit two call-wall trades and cut the rare convex winner that supplies most profile PnL.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call-Wall Convex Recovery Monitor
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path features: early MFE, half-TP touch timing, MAE expansion before TP, configured TP distance, profile id, and ES/VWAP state at entry.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: call_wall_acceptance_long enters with wide target or extended ES-VWAP and fails to convert early favorable movement into sustained progress before adverse expansion.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce global worst MAE from -655.2 toward <= -513.4, retain >=70% call-wall PnL, total net profit >=98% of leader, slow_12h remains 0, and score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate 1DTE pullback/reclaim behavior from the existing breakout fallback without damaging profitable breakout_long_1dte baseline.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router overlap may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or the companion may be unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: 1DTE option-map state, ES reclaim/pullback structure, existing breakout_long_1dte trade attribution, and router cannibalization report.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_1DTE_ONLY with 0DTE unavailable and ES state resembling pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1241.8, 1DTE average/worst MAE improves, breakout_long_1dte retained PnL is measured, total net profit >=98%, and score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dormant valid-0DTE pin-avoid concepts may absorb high-extension/pin-risk states currently entering through charm-pin or charm-wall profiles.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May produce no trades or steal profitable fast extended-VWAP winners from charm_pin_pullback_long.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Attribution Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router attribution, dormant profile standalone trades, protected fast/extended-VWAP winner retention, charm-pin/charm-wall cannibalization.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm-wall or charm-pin context near pin-risk conditions where current profiles admit tail-heavy entries.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated profile has positive standalone PnL or lowers combined charm/pin worst MAE, total net profit >=98%, protected fast-winner retention >=95%, and no new slow trades.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Pullback deep-heat entries are profitable but can tie capital and produce -403 MAE despite small TP.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Static gates may remove the largest PnL profile's normal recovery trades because pullback_long has broad profitable feature overlap.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Early-Progress Heat Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade first 5-15 minute MFE/MAE, first +1.5 point timing, MFE-before-MAE flag, profile id, and entry veto context.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: pullback_long entry fails to make early positive progress and adverse move expands before any meaningful MFE.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% pullback_long PnL, improve pullback worst MAE from -403.2, keep total PnL/hour flat or better, and slow_12h remains 0.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use the existing extended-VWAP rejection near-miss family as the first risk-reducer path. Prefer a narrow call-wall-aware or profile-aware boundary study around the exact failed thresholds rather than global ES-VWAP caps. Validate protected extended-VWAP winner retention explicitly.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce the -655.2 global MAE while keeping the call-wall winner class from being over-pruned.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst tail is a near miss of the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection signature; a small boundary nudge can catch the bad call-wall/pullback tails without broad throughput loss.
next_experiments.name: Profile-Aware Extended-VWAP Tail Repair
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE and risk-adjusted score, with total net profit >=98% of run_044/run_047 leader and protected extended-VWAP winner retention >=95%.
next_experiments.risk: The extended-VWAP region contains 18 protected winners and $1051.9 PnL, so broad extension filtering will likely damage the edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Constrain call_wall_acceptance_long realization risk before sample expansion. Bias toward smaller/earlier realization or admission containment only inside call_wall_acceptance_long; do not loosen DEX/call-volume gates globally.
next_experiments.goal: Keep call-wall convex PnL while lowering its profile MAE and hold-time tail.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The two-trade call-wall profile is profitable but overexposed to wide-target recovery; local containment can improve portfolio drawdown/MAE with minimal PnL loss.
next_experiments.name: Call-Wall Early Realization Containment
next_experiments.primary_metric: call_wall_acceptance_long worst MAE, global worst MAE, total score, and retained call-wall PnL >=70%.
next_experiments.risk: The worst trade is also the largest winner, so excessive TP compression can lower net profit more than it reduces risk.

next_experiments.config_bias: Study stale no-0DTE behavior separately from EXPIRED_0DTE. Bias toward stale fallback efficiency trims only if VALID_0DTE and the single EXPIRED_0DTE winner remain unchanged.
next_experiments.goal: Improve fallback efficiency without disturbing primary 0DTE flow.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE fallback trades are low-efficiency insurance and may be pruned or tightened without harming the main edge.
next_experiments.name: Stale No-0DTE State Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: STALE_0DTE PnL/hour, fallback worst MAE, unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL/trade count, and total net profit >=98%.
next_experiments.risk: Very small stale sample; removing fallback insurance may only look good in this short window.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep breakout_long_1dte thresholds stable. If legal, test pullback_long_1dte as a companion router split rather than direct tightening. Measure cannibalization of existing breakout_long_1dte trades.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce 1DTE average MAE while preserving the profitable 1DTE fallback contribution.
next_experiments.hypothesis: 1DTE fallback contains multiple ES states; a companion pullback profile can separate weaker pullback/reclaim entries from clean breakouts.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Split Validation
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average/worst MAE, breakout_long_1dte retained PnL/trades, and total score.
next_experiments.risk: Prior direct 1DTE tightening failed; companion routing must not simply block profitable breakout entries.

next_experiments.config_bias: Activate exactly one dormant valid-0DTE pin-avoid concept as an attribution experiment only, with a full router-list replacement and minimal same-profile tuning. Do not activate multiple dormant profiles in one test.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether existing dormant pin-avoid logic can route away tail-heavy charm/pin states without broad global vetoes.
next_experiments.hypothesis: A dormant pin-avoid profile may capture the tail geometry currently showing up as extended-VWAP/charm-wall/charm-pin near misses.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Attribution Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Activated profile standalone PnL, combined charm/pin worst MAE, protected fast-winner retention, total score.
next_experiments.risk: Dormant profiles may be unreachable or may cannibalize high-quality charm_pin_pullback_long fast winners.

next_experiments.config_bias: Freeze squeeze admissions and avoid TP compression while risk reduction focuses elsewhere. Use squeeze as a control edge; only accept changes that keep its worst MAE near -225 and PnL/hour near $200+.
next_experiments.goal: Use squeeze_long as a clean-edge control and avoid damaging it during portfolio risk reduction.
next_experiments.hypothesis: squeeze_long is not the current tail problem and should act as a stability check for router or global veto changes.
next_experiments.name: Squeeze Control Edge Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: squeeze_long PnL/hour, squeeze worst MAE, total score, and unchanged slow_12h count.
next_experiments.risk: Global veto or router changes can unintentionally remove squeeze continuation winners despite not targeting the profile.

research_notes: Do not emit config changes from this plan. The optimizer should translate only the experiment intent into legal config-only trials. The main risk target is not realized losses; it is hidden MAE and long recovery path. Avoid repeating rejected upstream changes such as indicator-register-resolution, ema-period, broad runtime exits, broad pullback TP/VWAP expansion, or direct 1DTE tightening. Broad single-threshold candidates are weak because they either leave worst MAE unchanged or retain too little PnL. The best current research direction is surgical profile-aware tail containment around call_wall_acceptance_long and extended-VWAP near-misses, with strict protected-winner retention checks.
risk_controls: Require total net profit retention >=98% of run_044/run_047 leader for any risk-reducer promotion unless worst MAE improves materially below -513.4.
Reject any experiment that introduces slow >=12h trades; current leader family has zero slow trades.
Track protected fast_winners, low_mae_winners, high_pnl_winners, and extended_vwap_winners by entry UTC; do not promote filters that block more than 5% without a large MAE improvement.
Measure profile cannibalization for every router or dormant activation experiment, especially against pullback_long, charm_pin_pullback_long, squeeze_long, and breakout_long_1dte.
Treat call_wall_acceptance_long as tail-heavy small-sample: require both profile-level and portfolio-level MAE improvement before expanding it.
Keep direct 1DTE breakout gates stable because prior direct tightening hurt score and net profit.
Separate STALE_0DTE from EXPIRED_0DTE when evaluating fallback changes; do not remove the single expired fallback winner based on stale evidence.
Use robust leader run_029 family as the risk benchmark: worst MAE -513.4 and drawdown 0.4%, while run_044/run_047 are higher-PnL but worse-tail variants.
